hero.label: Made for Stamp
hero.headline: I want to help Stamp turn inbox chaos into revenue.
hero.subheadline: Stamp is tackling a problem almost everyone feels every day, but with a product that feels genuinely new. I am excited by the chance to help Archit build the early revenue engine for an AI-native email client that can change how people work.

whyCompany.title: Stamp rethinks email as an autonomous workflow
whyCompany.body: Most email tools help people manage inboxes better, but Stamp is aiming to automate the work itself through prioritization, filtering, summaries, and action tracking. That makes the product feel much bigger than productivity software because it saves attention, time, and execution.

whyCompany.title: An early YC moment with real go-to-market upside
whyCompany.body: Stamp is still extremely early, which makes this the perfect moment to shape how the company talks to users, tests channels, and builds repeatable revenue motion. Joining now means learning directly from the founder while helping define the first sales playbook, feedback loops, and customer journey.

whyCompany.title: I have already built pipeline from zero
whyCompany.body: At Copygen.ai, I ran cold outreach, LinkedIn sequences, and HubSpot workflows that helped bring in 15 to 20 paying clients. At NextAfter, I strengthened my digital marketing foundation in a B2B consulting environment, which gave me a better understanding of messaging, positioning, and how to connect product value to buyer pain.

quote: I want this role because Stamp is not just selling another inbox tool, it is selling back people's focus. That is the kind of product I would be excited to learn, pitch, and help grow every day.
